MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alicia Gamez, Esq. 415-341-8143 Defendant in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALICIA GAMEZ'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendant Alicia M. Gamez's motion to strike the complaint is moot in light of the court's ruling granting defendant's motion to quash service of summons.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jun-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alicia Gamez, Esq. 415-341-8143 Defendant in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALICIA GAMEZ's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT.

Defendant Alicia M. Gamez's motion to quash service of summons and complaint is granted. The alternative motion to dismiss pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 389 (b) is moot in light of the court's ruling granting defendant's motion to quash service of summons.

According to Ms. Gomez's declaration, she is providing legal services as a natural person and "the Law Office of Alicia Gamez" is a fictitious name under which she is doing business but is not a separate legal entity. (Gamez Decl. 3(c)(d); see Providence Washington Ins. Co. v. Valley Forge Ins. Co. (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 1194, 1200 ["Doing business under another name does not create an entity distinct from the person operating the business. The business name is a fiction, and so too is any implication that the business is a legal entity separate from its owner."]; Ball v. Steadfast-BLK (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 694, 701.
Failure to attempt personal service on an individual defendant before effecting substitute service falls short of substantial compliance with the service statutes. (Code Civ. Proc. 415.20(b); Local Rule 6.6(c); see Evartt v. Superior Court (1979) 89 Cal.App.3d 795, 802 ["substitute abode service on petitioner was ineffective and void because real party failed to comply with the mandatory prerequisite to such service by exercising reasonable diligence to effect personal service."].) Furthermore, under Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 415, leaving the complaint with defendant's legal assistant, who was not authorized to accept service on behalf of defendant, does not constitute substantial compliance with the statute. (Gamez Decl. 3(e)).

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
